title: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Bedford, OH
metaTitle: Section 8 Apartments for Rent in Bedford, OH
metaDescription: Looking for Section 8 apartments for rent in Bedford, Ohio? See how CMHA vouchers work in Cuyahoga County and find voucher-friendly homes. Book a showing.
primaryKeyword: section 8 apartments for rent Bedford ohio
answerBox: Bedford, Ohio sits in Cuyahoga County, where the Housing Choice Voucher program is administered by CMHA. Some Bedford landlords accept Section 8, but Ohio has no statewide law requiring it, and Bedford is not one of the few Cuyahoga cities with a voucher-protection ordinance. Rent Finder Cleveland works with voucher-ready rental homes across Greater Cleveland — tell us what you need.

sections.h2: Are there Section 8 apartments for rent in Bedford, Ohio?
body: Bedford is a Cuyahoga County suburb located southeast of Cleveland. Because Bedford sits inside Cuyahoga County, its renters fall under the same Housing Choice Voucher program administered countywide by the Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA) — the same agency that serves the city of Cleveland and every other Cuyahoga suburb. Whether a specific Bedford apartment accepts a voucher, however, comes down to the individual landlord, since acceptance is not mandated everywhere in the county.

Rent Finder Cleveland's current rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland proper — mainly the east and southeast side neighborhoods — rather than in Bedford itself. Every home we work with accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready, so if a Cleveland location could work for your voucher search, [book a showing](/p/book-showing) and tell us what you're looking for.

sections.h2: Does Bedford protect Section 8 voucher holders from discrimination?
body: No — as of this writing, Bedford is not one of the Cuyahoga County cities with a source-of-income protection ordinance. Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, so acceptance is left to each landlord and each city's own rules. Only a handful of Cuyahoga suburbs — Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale — currently bar landlords from refusing an applicant for using a voucher, and Bedford is not among them.

That means a Bedford landlord can lawfully decline to rent to a voucher holder for that reason alone. See our full breakdown of [how source-of-income protections work in Cuyahoga County](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/source-of-income-discrimination-cleveland-ohio/) for the current patchwork of local rules and how to check a specific city's ordinance before you tour. This is general information, not legal advice — always verify current law with the city.

sections.h2: How the CMHA voucher process works for Cuyahoga County renters
body: CMHA accepts preliminary Housing Choice Voucher applications year-round, with no closing date, through its online [SecureCafe application portal](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php). You'll need an active email address, Social Security numbers, and the names, dates of birth, and income information for everyone in your household. Selection off the list happens through periodic mini-lotteries rather than strict first-come, first-served order, so an invitation can arrive within days or take considerably longer.

Your household also has to fall under CMHA's income limits, shown below for a few common household sizes (effective April 3, 2025 — CMHA updates these figures periodically, so confirm the current limit before applying).

sections.h2: What does rent look like for voucher holders near Bedford?
body: Cleveland-area rents give a useful benchmark even if you're searching from Bedford. Per Zumper's July 2026 report, the Cleveland metro's median asking rent is $1,250 a month, with three-bedroom units averaging about $1,350. RentCafe's July 2026 data, which leans toward larger professionally managed buildings, showed a higher $1,564 average. Actual rent for any specific Bedford address will vary by building, condition, and landlord.

Rent Finder Cleveland's own selection of voucher-ready rental homes runs roughly $700 to $1,800 a month, typically around $1,000, mostly two- and three-bedroom homes. Under a voucher, your share of that rent is based on your income and CMHA's payment standard for the unit's bedroom size, not the full asking rent.

Utility responsibility also affects your bottom line: CMHA factors a utility allowance into your calculation when a tenant pays for heat, electric, or water separately from rent, so two apartments with the same asking rent can still cost a voucher household different amounts out of pocket. See our [Section 8 utility allowance explainer](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/section-8-utility-allowance-cleveland/) for how that works before you compare a Bedford listing to a Cleveland one.

sections.h2: Questions worth asking before you tour near Bedford
body: Because Bedford landlords decide individually whether to accept a voucher, it helps to confirm a few things before you schedule a showing anywhere in Cuyahoga County.

items: **Does this specific unit accept Housing Choice Vouchers, in writing?** A general willingness to consider vouchers isn't the same as a confirmed yes for that address.
**Has the unit passed a CMHA housing quality inspection before, or will one need to be scheduled?** Assistance can't start until the unit clears inspection.
**How does the landlord handle a gap between the payment standard and the asking rent?** Some units simply cost more than your voucher covers.
**Does the move-in timeline fit your voucher's search window?** Ask CMHA directly about your deadline and any extension options.

sections.h2: Renting a voucher-friendly home with Rent Finder Cleveland
body: We work with more than 90 rental homes across Greater Cleveland, and every single one accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready — no exceptions. Current inventory is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods such as Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker, plus a smaller number of homes on the west side and in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria.

If you hold a voucher and a specific Bedford address isn't a strict requirement, see the full picture in our [Cleveland Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) — it covers current neighborhoods, rent ranges, and how to get started.

faq.q: Do you work with Section 8 apartments in Bedford, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, plus a few homes in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria. Every home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers, so if a nearby Cleveland location would work for your search, book a showing and tell us what you need.

faq.q: Is Bedford required to accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: No. Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and Bedford is not one of the Cuyahoga County cities with a local source-of-income protection ordinance. Acceptance is decided landlord by landlord, so always ask directly before touring a unit.

faq.q: How do I apply for a Housing Choice Voucher if I live near Bedford?
faq.a: Apply through CMHA's online portal, which covers all of Cuyahoga County including Bedford. You'll need an active email address, Social Security numbers, and income details for your household. Selection happens by periodic mini-lottery rather than strict order, so timing varies from days to months.

faq.q: What income limits apply for a Bedford household?
faq.a: CMHA's income limits, effective April 3, 2025, are $34,800 for one person, $39,800 for two, $49,700 for four, and $65,650 for eight, with limits in between for other household sizes. These figures update periodically, so confirm the current limit with CMHA before applying.

faq.q: Can I use my voucher on a Rent Finder Cleveland home instead of one in Bedford?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Our homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side, so if that area works for you, book a showing to see current availability.
